title: Completion, chat, and tool-calling APIs
description: Providers expose raw completion, role-structured chat, and tool/function-calling surfaces that differ in how instructions and outputs are framed.
body: Use when choosing or migrating which API surface a call uses. Raw text completion takes one prompt and returns text; chat APIs structure input as system/user/assistant messages so instructions and data can be separated; tool-calling lets the model emit structured calls against declared schemas instead of free text. Do not assume behavior transfers unchanged when migrating surfaces, and avoid stuffing system rules into a user message where injection can reach them. Actions: pick the surface that lets you separate instructions from data and obtain structure natively; verify how each frames stop sequences and max-tokens. Validation signals include tests that the chosen surface keeps system instructions isolated and returns parseable structure. Risk boundary: migrating surfaces changes output distribution and injection exposure, so route it through evaluation, not a silent swap.
